title: Verbる べくもない
titleEn: Means 'it's impossible to...' or 'there's no way to...'.
explanation: Means 'it's impossible to...' or 'there's no way to...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point is used when you want to express that something is impossible or there is no way to do something. The verb before 'べくもない' should be in its dictionary form.

title: Verbる までもない
titleEn: It's not necessary to the extent of; no need to.
explanation: It's not necessary to the extent of; no need to. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜るまでもない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessary to the extent of doing that action. It can be translated as 'no need to' in English.

title: Verbる ものとする
explanation: Assuming or deciding something as... (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～るものとする grammar point is often used in formal or legal contexts to express a decision or assumption. It can be translated as 'it is decided/assumed that...' or 'shall be deemed that...'.

title: Verbる や否や
titleEn: Expresses the idea of 'as soon as', 'the moment...'.
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'as soon as', 'the moment...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point Verbる や否や is used to express something happening immediately after another action or event. It can be translated as 'as soon as...' or 'the moment...'. The verb must be in dictionary form.

title: Verbる 始末だ
titleEn: Indicates a (often unfortunate) result or conclusion of an action.
explanation: Indicates a (often unfortunate) result or conclusion of an action. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～る始末だ grammar point expresses the outcome or conclusion of an action, typically a negative or unfortunate result. It can be translated as 'end up...', 'the result is...', or 'the conclusion is...'.

title: Verbる 嫌いがある
titleEn: Tendency to do a negative action; have a bad habit of ~.
explanation: Tendency to do a negative action; have a bad habit of ~. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～る嫌いがある grammar point is used to express a negative tendency or habit. It often carries a critical or disapproving tone and points out someone's habitual action.

title: いつまで～のやら
titleEn: Expresses uncertainty regarding duration; 'don't know when... will end'.
structure: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら
structureEn: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら
explanation: Expresses uncertainty regarding duration; 'don't know when... will end'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The いつまで～のやら grammar point is used to express uncertainty about when something will end or finish. It is often used in situations where the speaker is unsure about when a particular state of affairs or event will conclude.

title: ～が Verb られる
titleEn: Expresses potential or ability to do something; 'can', 'able to'.
structure:
❶ (Group 1) Verb:  書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc.
❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる)
structureEn:
❶ (Group 1) Verb:  書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc.
❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる)
explanation: Expresses potential or ability to do something; 'can', 'able to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～が Verb られる grammar pattern generally refers to the potential form of Japanese verbs (i.e., 'can do something'). However, the exact formation depends on the verb group. For most Group 1 (五段) verbs, potential form changes the final -u to -eru (e.g., 書く→書ける). For Group 2 (一段) verbs, you often see ～られる (e.g., 食べる→食べられる). In English, it translates as 'can' or 'able to'.

title: ～かと思いきや
titleEn: Expressing unexpected outcomes; 'just when I thought...', 'but...'.
structure: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや)
structureEn: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや)
explanation: Expressing unexpected outcomes; 'just when I thought...', 'but...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かと思いきや grammar point is used when the outcome turns out different from what was initially assumed or expected. It can be translated as 'just when I thought...' or simply 'but (actually)...'. It often highlights that the speaker’s assumption was proven wrong by subsequent events.

title: ～がゆえに
titleEn: Expresses the reason or cause; 'because', 'due to', 'as a result of'.
structure: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに
structureEn: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに
explanation: Expresses the reason or cause; 'because', 'due to', 'as a result of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～がゆえに grammar point is used to express a reason or cause, often in more formal writing or speech. It roughly corresponds to 'because (of)', 'due to', or 'as a result of' in English.
